title: ipomoea contorquens var. vulgaris
intro: Ipomoea contorquens var. vulgaris, commonly known as the Moonflower or Ipomoea, is a perennial vine in the Convolvulaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which provide its therapeutic benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Ipomoea contorquens var. vulgaris include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
